name: serialize_injective_without_marshalable
reason: The trait-level `lemma_serialize_injective` is external_body requiring `is_marshalable` for both arguments, but for usize the impl's external_body has no preconditions — it may assert injectivity even for non-marshalable values (usize > u64::MAX) where `ghost_serialize` is underspecified

name: ghost_serialize_trait_uninterpreted_collision
reason: None serializes as `seq![0]` (1 byte) while Some(empty) serializes as `seq![1] + usize_serialize(0)` (9 bytes) — this should hold, but `ghost_serialize` is external_body at the trait level, so the open spec impls must actually be used for this to verify

name: view_equal_not_transitive_option

proof fn phi_3_view_equal_not_transitive_option(a: Option<Vec<u8>>, b: Option<Vec<u8>>, c: Option<Vec<u8>>)

        a.view_equal(&b),
        b.view_equal(&c),

        a.view_equal(&c),
{

reason: `view_equal` is used as an equivalence relation in `lemma_serialize_injective` but transitivity is never axiomatized — if an impl's `view_equal` is not transitive, chaining injectivity results would be unsound

name: serialize_injective_implies_view_not_equality
code:
proof fn phi_4_serialize_injective_implies_view_not_equality(a: u64, b: u64)
    requires
        a.is_marshalable(),
        b.is_marshalable(),
        a.view_equal(&b),
    ensures
        a == b,
{
}
reason: For u64, `view_equal` is defined as `self@ === other@` which should imply actual equality — but if the SMT solver derives `a == b` from `view_equal` for all types via the external_body injectivity lemma, it could over-constrain types where view_equal is intentionally weaker than structural equality

name: tuple_serialize_prefix_ambiguity
reason: Tuple serialization concatenates without length prefixes — the external_body injectivity lemma asserts `view_equal` from equal serializations, but splitting the concatenated bytes back into components requires fixed-length first elements; if the first element has variable length, the split point is ambiguous
